event: Navy underwater drone mine clearance ops in Strait of Hormuz drive 6-8 week lead time spike for waterproof motor/ESC combos — U.S. Central Command announced underwater drone deployment for Iranian mine clearing, validating demand for sealed propulsion systems tracked in Forge DB (165 ESCs, 219 batteries)
evidence_basis: CENTCOM April 11 announcement of underwater drone mine clearance in Hormuz + Forge DB tracking 165 ESCs (119 China-origin, 26 USA) and 219 batteries — operational tempo implies procurement acceleration for waterproof variants
probability_basis: Strong operational signal from named CENTCOM announcement, but lead time estimate is inferred from typical procurement cycles — no primary source on timing
drivers: U.S. Navy underwater drone mine clearance announced for Strait of Hormuz
165 ESCs in Forge DB with 72% China-origin concentration creates supply risk

lead_time_signal.component: waterproof ESCs and sealed battery systems

event: C-UAS laser targeting sensor demand accelerates Q2-Q3 2026 — FAA-DoD border agreement for high-energy lasers implies procurement of targeting payloads (FLIR Boson 640, Teledyne FLIR sensors) for border deployment following Feb 25 friendly-fire incident
evidence_basis: Pentagon/FAA April 10 landmark safety agreement on laser C-UAS deployment at US-Mexico border + existing FLIR Boson 640 cascade flag (19 platforms, 7 programs, SINGLE SOURCE Teledyne FLIR) — laser systems require targeting sensors
probability_basis: Named agreement from DoD/FAA primary source validates deployment, but sensor procurement link is strong inference based on operational requirements — no itemized BOM provided
drivers: FAA-DoD border laser C-UAS safety agreement finalized April 10, 2026
High-energy laser systems require thermal/targeting sensors — FLIR Boson 640 already flagged as critical single-source component

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: C-UAS laser systems co-occur with EW tagging in 3 of 4 recent articles — suggests integrated counter-drone approach driving multi-modal sensor demand

event: Validation: Ukrainian drone maker Paris meetings signal European component reshoring timeline H2 2026–2027 — 24+ Ukrainian firms seeking French co-production deals, but only 1 French joint venture vs 11 German, 5 Spanish confirms slow EU integration pace
evidence_basis: April 10 Paris Ukrainian Council of Defence Industry briefing — CEO Ihor Fedirko cited 1 French JV vs 11 German, 5 Spanish — validates slower timeline than existing Netherlands €248M prediction implied
probability_basis: Primary source from named Ukrainian defense industry CEO provides factual JV counts, but H2 2026–2027 timeline is inferred from pace of existing German/Spanish deals — France lag is credible signal
drivers: Ukrainian Council of Defence Industry CEO confirms only 1 French JV vs 11 German JVs as of April 10
24+ Ukrainian firms active in Paris signals intent, but French bureaucratic pace historically slower than German Ringtausch model

event: Hezbollah FPV drone ramp validates China-origin ESC/FC supply pressure — video evidence of commercial FPV components in Lebanon combat accelerates DoD scrutiny of 328 flight controllers (81% STM32) and 165 ESCs (72% China-origin) in Forge DB
evidence_basis: April 10 TWZ report on Hezbollah FPV attacks on IDF armor + existing critical flag that 267/328 FCs use STM32 and 119/165 ESCs are China-origin — validates dual-use component diversion risk
probability_basis: TWZ reporting on Hezbollah FPV use is credible military analysis source, and Forge DB component counts are verified data — probability reflects confidence that Lebanon combat footage drives DoD procurement review, not a statistical model
drivers: Hezbollah ramping FPV drone attacks on IDF armor per April 10 TWZ combat footage analysis
Forge DB shows 119 of 165 ESCs are China-origin (72%), creating NDAA compliance gap if DoD accelerates domestic FPV procurement

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: FPV and one-way attack tags co-occur in 1 of 7 articles — Hezbollah use case mirrors Ukraine Geran/Shahed diversion pattern, suggesting commercial component scrutiny intensifies

event: Bahrain F-16 Block 70 Iranian drone shootdowns create no component cascade — air-to-air intercept of slow drones does not validate FPV component supply chains, but confirms kinetic C-UAS remains primary for state actors vs laser/EW for asymmetric threats
evidence_basis: April 10 TWZ report on Royal Bahraini Air Force F-16D Block 70 scoring first air-to-air kills of Iranian drones — no component linkage to Forge DB, but validates divergent C-UAS procurement strategies (kinetic vs directed energy)
probability_basis: Credible TWZ reporting on F-16 shootdowns, but supply chain impact is low confidence — included because it clarifies that state-level C-UAS (kinetic intercept) diverges from border/asymmetric C-UAS (laser/EW), affecting procurement priorities
drivers: Bahrain F-16 Block 70 first air-to-air drone kills validates kinetic intercept for state threats
Diverges from FAA-DoD laser C-UAS for border — suggests dual procurement tracks: kinetic for peer/near-peer, directed energy for asymmetric

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: C-UAS appears in 5 of 7 articles but EW only in 3 — kinetic intercept (F-16, Coyote) vs EW/laser split emerging in procurement

event: Coyote counter-drone launcher expansion to USS Carl M. Levin validates Raytheon production ramp — mystery launcher likely Coyote system (per USS Bainbridge, USS Churchill precedent), implies 3+ destroyers now equipped, drives demand for Coyote Block 2+ airframes and launchers
evidence_basis: April 10 TWZ report on USS Carl M. Levin mystery launcher + existing validation prediction citing USS Bainbridge and USS Winston S. Churchill Coyote deployments — third ship confirms multi-vessel integration, not test phase
probability_basis: TWZ visual identification of launcher + precedent of two named destroyers with Coyote systems makes third ship highly probable — Raytheon production ramp is strong inference from multi-ship deployment
drivers: USS Carl M. Levin mystery launcher matches Coyote integration pattern on USS Bainbridge and USS Winston S. Churchill
Three destroyers equipped suggests transition from test to fleet-wide C-UAS modernization — Raytheon Coyote Block 2+ production acceleration implied
